description: A sensor-fusion estimator that diverges or freezes when a measurement is dropped or delayed because it lacks timeout and outlier handling.
body: Failure mode: the state estimator assumes measurements always arrive on time, so when a sensor drops out or sends a stale/outlier reading the estimate either keeps integrating a bad value or stops correcting and drifts, then feeds a wrong state into control. Detection signals: the estimate diverges from ground truth after a dropped packet; covariance does not grow when measurements stop; an outlier reading jerks the estimate. Do not assume measurements are timely or valid. Safer fallback: weight each measurement by its covariance and timestamp, widen uncertainty when a sensor times out instead of trusting stale data, reject outliers, and test that a dropped measurement degrades the estimate gracefully rather than corrupting it.
